search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae abdominal pain adults telehealth clinical guideline PDF
site:nice.org.uk abdominal pain adults referral guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The presentation does not establish a surgical diagnosis.
Treatment and medicine content was not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults; the legacy workflow has no age restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: direct for DHA-licensed telehealth assessment and referral, not every in-person or emergency abdominal-pain context.
UAE_applicability: Direct Dubai DHA guidance for the mapped telehealth fields.
recency_verification: The current DHA Version 2 Issue 2 adult abdominal-pain guideline was verified as the official copy and remains within its 2029 revision period.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult abdominal-pain identity and matching terms within DHA telehealth.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines adult abdominal-pain use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped onset, location, duration, character, radiation, meals or bowels, nausea or vomiting, bowel, urinary, fever, surgery, medication, presets, and structured history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped GI bleeding, vomiting, severe pain, fever, guarding or rebound, peritoneal, weight-loss, jaundice, and assessed-negative fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed blood, urinalysis, and imaging fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped safety-netting and clinician-decided referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children and non-telehealth settings are outside the exact applicability statement.

Broad GI pain alias, bowel-sound and general vital fields, abdominal-mass label, previous-record review, generic clinician plan, generic follow-up, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some examination fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, differential diagnosis, investigation selection or interpretation, treatment, urgency, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
